assertions.text: The driver pulled away (started moving) before accelerating for about ten seconds.
The vehicle accelerated for about ten seconds up to roughly 45 km/h.
A single impact occurred (one distinct collision).
The impact happened while the vehicle was accelerating (i.e., during the ten-second acceleration period).
After the collision the vehicle slowed and rolled to a near stop (braking or deceleration occurred after impact).
After the collision the vehicle came to a near stop (final speed near zero after rolling post-impact).

verdicts.evidence: The GPS speed is [0.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) then rises to [5.0 km/h at t=2.0 s] and continues increasing, showing the vehicle started moving before the acceleration period described (first non-zero speed at [2.0 s with 5.0 km/h](T3)).
GPS speed rises from [5.0 km/h at t=2.0 s] to [45.0 km/h at t=10.0 s], a period of about [8.0 s] between those samples (and from t=0 to t=10 is [10.0 s]); the peak GPS speed reaches [47.0 km/h](T2).

Detect_events found a single major impact spike on accel_x at [t=11.01 s with magnitude 5.86 g](T4) along with one spike on accel_y at [t=11.01 s with magnitude 2.67 g](T4), both co-timed indicating one collision event rather than multiple separate impacts.

The acceleration channel shows a large impact spike with peak of [5.86 g at t=11.01 s](T4) and an integrated device-frame delta-V on accel_x of [−54.4 km/h over the window](T2), consistent with a heavy impact signature.

The GPS speed was increasing and reaches [45.0 km/h at t=10.0 s](T3) and the impact spike occurs at [t=11.01 s](T4), within about [1.0 s] after that peak and within the described acceleration window (t=0–10 s described), indicating the impact coincides with the end of the acceleration period.

After the impact at [t=11.01 s](T4) GPS speed falls from [47.0 km/h at t≈11 s] to [22.0 km/h by t=13.0 s] (a drop of [23.0 km/h] detected) and continues to [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s], showing deceleration/rolling toward a near stop (speed drop [23.0 km/h between t=10.0–13.0 s](T4), and final [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T3)).
The final recorded GPS speed is [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T3), indicating the vehicle rolled to a near stop after the collision.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle started from zero, accelerated up to about [45–47 km/h](T3)(T2) over roughly [10 s](T3), experienced a single large impact spike at [t=11.01 s with 5.86 g](T4) and then decelerated to near zero ([2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T3)), supporting the narrative.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
